item.title: My favorite line...
item.content:encoded:
“We are all dumb until we know, and then we are a little more dumb.“
Yes, just yes.
I have upvoted enthusiastically!
I have this on my “read offline” list to just pick up for inspiration when I get stuck; that’s how unique this is.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
The image that was used in this article has been replaced with an image that's cc-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) and is sourced as follows:

Title: fruit.png
Author(s): Rhododendrites, faminepulse
Release year: 2020
Note: Created by faminepulse using the image below.

Source: Wikimedia Commons
License: CC BY-SA 4.0
Title: Raspberry with fuzz (40969).jpg
Author: Rhododendrites

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Great dialogue and characterization. The whole thing is entertaining but somehow eerie.
Although it seems kind of unlikely that we're the only ones to fantasised about time travel. Yet I don't know if it's a case of Humans are Stupid or Humans are Special trope. In a sense it would make as very imaginative.
One of my favourites. +++1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I read the comments of this occasionally and it makes me so grateful for the SCP community. There is nothing else like it and the guidance it provided me when I was not worth a damn writing led me to praise like this. I hope this community lives on for a long time and other aspiring writers can find themselves here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: SCP-2000 offered more as a building block to the SCP canon that people can use to shape future stories within that universe. Looking back at 2000 today makes the context of things like red Sea object more interesting than what this could offer as a stand alone story.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Shouldn't this be tagged pattern-screamer? As I understand it, this was written before they got a tag, but there are too many explicit "screaming" references with thematic similarities to pattern screamers here that it fits. Maybe faminepulse will see this and clarify.

Either way, this was my 2nd fave entry in 2000-con a few years ago, and it still is one of my fave SCPs today.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
and a 70 m x 5 m containment cell

Isn't this missing a dimension…?

item.title: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded:
Noting that revision 37 by gobarn has been reverted, due to it being a major change without staff or authorial permission.

While 7 meters is much more reasonable than 70, I believe that measurement was included due to the object's area of effect radius. Staff who disagree with the revert may reinstate the edit per consensus with other staff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is amazing and lovely and amazing. You should feel great, famine.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Spotted a mistake in one of the logs - "the" is repeated:

"My purpose is to gather knowledge. I will find the the roots of infinity at the end of the Mesh"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a strong contender for the greatest thing ever written on the wiki. Not much more I can say…this is stunning.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The only possible conclusion, Raspberries!
Oh, why can't i upvote it twice…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Probably one of the best scips written. Somehow so alien, yet still likable, polite, and relatable. Loved it, like many others.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I thoroughly enjoyed reading this SCP. While you don’t rely on the typical horror that many of other entries to the wiki seem to, I really think that you capitalize on the unknown in an interesting way. With some other SCP’s the foundation is, for lack of a better phrase, in the dark to some degree; here they certainly don’t know everything but the raspberry knows less about the situation. It’s refreshing to see you play with that dynamic in a way that I haven’t seen elsewhere on the wiki.

Is it perfect? No. But the gripes I have are small and have been covered already in the discussion by other people.

Kudos, upvote well earned.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was really, really, REALLY good.

You also instantly sealed my vote when the thing looked through the fourth wall and got pissed that I was laughing at how dumb it was. And then ate itself.

(Also, this reminded me of Earthbound's Gygas, which was ALSO based of Azathoth, but I ran into Super Nintento rpgs before Lovecraft, soooo…)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a very interesting portrait of a potential destiny of a civilization that transcends into a singularity. The upgraded-navel-gazing at the end was particularly satisfying. I like it and am upvoting, but do have one minor quibble:

I do not like the use of the word "snippet" in a professional document. Snippets are things you find in Facebook posts and scrapbooks. "Excerpt" would be a far better word to use.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's no relation, those elements are just kind of a motif.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I enjoyed this.

But what is relation between 2682 and Níðhǫggr?
I do not get it no matter how much I think about it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fucking raspberries man. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This post makes me think of something the Tenth Doctor said in the episode Utopia, upon finding a large group of humans living together, in a time where the Universe is about to die off.

The ripe old smell of humans. You survive. Oh, you might have spent a million years evolving into clouds of gas and another million as downloads, but you always revert to the same basic shape. The fundamental human. End of the universe and here you are. Indomitable, that's the word. Indomitable!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
To be fair I am just now getting into Dr. Who. :P

These are all completely fair arguments. I never expected it to do as well as it did.

This isn't about a truly alien alien or its perception of us or our perception of it. I'm not married to the prose either as I still have a lot to learn in that area. Those were happy accidents that a lot of people on the wiki were fairly receptive to.

I had one method while writing this: Keep guessing at what comes next. What happens after we're immortal transhumans? After we master our universe? After we master others? After that? Keep going. After that? Guess. Can it even go any farther or did the Idiot just end itself?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a really popular article, so I'm clearly in the minority, but I don't really like this at all. I think it's quite well written, but it seems to take from the "Dr. Who" school of sci-fi, which is random technobabble, silly stuff, and aliens talking alternatively in dramatic poetry and quirky comedic anecdotes. I also didn't see it as that original. The whole "alien is confused by earth" and "super advanced alien society talks down to humans" tropes have been used a lot, and while they aren't yet cliches I didn't see anything particularly new or interesting about this. There were some smidgens of good stuff in there, but I don't know if this is trying to be interesting or comedic, and for me it was neither.

-1, unless I missed something (which is entirely possible).

item.title: Dat handwave tho
item.content:encoded: I did what I could, time in its case refers to old matter. I think it can differentiate between its concept of "time" and ours. Thanks for pointing that out though!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Uhm. What happened to the image?

E: I found the same image on the SCP-KO wiki. Uploaded it here for the sake of making the article look good, with staff permission.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I just read this again and I love it so much.

I think the thing I love the most is that it was trapped by flypaper accidentally. It pushed through to a new universe it didn't understand and so the unfathomable alien thing was defeated by something equally alien and unfathomable to it… at least until it figured out raspberries and once it had one piece of the puzzle in hand it understood how to put everything together. Once it understood our world it was free.

(glad it didn't eat us too, that was polite)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The picture for this article is dead. Because you linked it from your sandbox. ._.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My only quibble with this is its views on time.

Not that it couldn't understand what time was, that was brilliant. But later down the logs, it starts referring to time as a thing, which kind of breaks that initial brilliant set up. Still, enthusiastic +1 on the massive +1 pile.

item.title: Re: +200
item.content:encoded:
On the bright side, only four people have ever downvoted this article. Of those four, two of them have never contributed anything to the site, and a third went inactive four months ago.
I personally loved it. I'd like to see that ending sequence made into a movie, then hijacked by The Lazor Collection. BLAAAAAAAAAAAARRRRRRRGGGGGHHHH!

This is my first 200, so I thought I would just say woot here.

W-… engh.
Congrats, this SCP definitely deserves this rating. I love how you actually managed to portray what a legitimate extra-dimensional entity would sound like.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: wut

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well I understood maybe a third of that. What I did understand was funny though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i think they mean that they prefer this as an article, but think Deus Ex Machina fits more as 2000

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wait… you'd rather Deus Ex Machina end up as SCP-2000, but you prefer this? Someone please explain to me how this works.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow, I actually felt sad for the thing. Upvoted

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, would you look at that! You scored third place, and got a pretty good spot ! 2682.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is excellent. One of the most inventive and entertaining, if not frightening by certain implications, SCPs I've read in a while.

Also, when the eyes formed in the final addendum, the mental image I had nearly made me shit myself. Upvoted so very hard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ow. My brain hurts now. Thanks. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this. It's hard making a sapient non-human really sound alien, much less extra-dimensional. I'd rather Deus Ex Machina end up as SCP-2000, but I prefer this. Nice mix of funny/creepy/dark/uplifting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is absolutely the best candidate for SCP-2000 that I've come across so far and I've read almost all of them as of this comment. This is stunning and I feel like you've nailed the alien communication. As I was reading, I found myself believing. You've made me a believer, so please take this measly upvote as thanks. Awe-inspiring. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it! +1
The wall of text was intimidating though

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The whole thing left me with my head tilted. I could not understand a thing. I think this is the one article that left me clueless.

Thank you. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fruit of Stupidity, more like :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Woah. This is really, really alien.

A description of strange events that almost makes sense, followed by the damn thing eating itself. Sooo weeeiird … +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's this thing called "Reply". Try it. You might like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That was an idea, but I didn't feel like it was entirely appropriate to the skip. It's not actually a raspberry, and as far as we're concerned it's not very knowledgeable.

I remembered the line "Blind Idiot God" from when I was reading something Lovecrafty. I think "Blind Idiot" is a good depiction of the skip and a good allusion in reference to Azathoth, except that the concept of the multiverse entity is turned on its head.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Lovecraft reference.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So why is this not titled "Fruit of Knowledge"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not sure how to number what it said any differently…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

Upvoted with pleasure. My new favorite 2000. I love Lovecraft. I love Arthur C. Clarke. I love truly alien aliens, I love mythology, and I think you did this whole thing perfectly. I truly hope you win this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The entity said "one-thousand three thousand and fifty five", not "one-thousand three hundred and fifty five"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why the extra 0 in "1,355"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I read this entire thing in a confused Slavic accent

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How long did it take you to think up "Woggletonianites?"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was tempted to scroll down and downvote after all the nonsense about "acquiring all knowledge and getting thuper bored" and stuff about Limbo and Meshes and Chaos and the occult. Then the logs came, and I realized how stupid I was for taking much of the words at their literal meaning.

It's like the aliens in Arthur C. Clarke's Space Odyssey series, only not completely undone and rendered petty and anthropomorphic by the final sequel.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like the last log. I can relate to the frustration of trying to understand something new and the triumph that comes when you finally find the touchstone brings everything else into focus.
In this case, that touchstone was raspberries.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah… this is pretty damned good.

There were moments I didn't like, but instantly afterwards there would be something brilliant and so all is well. It is now my favourite 2000.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
To dear Crayne: neither do I.

To dear Antler creature: I didn't know there was 2 more weks to vote.

In summary: I'm a huge ignorance statement.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This tickles my fancy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That could have happened… A lot of things could have happened. I wouldn't give the thing too much credit with understanding how "consuming" something works.
